title: RevocationCheckMode
description: Behaviour when performing a revocation check during credential verification. Used in TrustProfile.revocation_policy.check_mode and OrganizationTrustProfile.revocation_policy.check_mode.

HARD_FAIL.description: Reject the credential if the revocation check fails for any reason (network error, parse failure, timeout). This is the safest mode and is required for ICAO and AAMVA high-assurance use cases.
HARD_FAIL.use_case: Border control, age verification for regulated access, financial credentials

SOFT_FAIL.description: Accept the credential with a warning flag if the revocation check is unavailable (e.g. network unreachable, endpoint timeout). The credential is accepted but the verification result includes a revocation_check_skipped flag.
SOFT_FAIL.use_case: Offline kiosk scenarios, low-assurance verifications, degraded network environments

SKIP.description: Bypass revocation checking entirely. Credential is accepted regardless of its revocation status. Should only be used in development/test environments.
SKIP.use_case: Development, testing, scenarios where credentials have no revocation endpoint
